title: Airopsis minuta
intro: Airopsis minuta, also known as dwarf club-rush, is a small, perennial plant belonging to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). Traditionally, Airopsis minuta has been used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, respiratory problems, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents of Airopsis minuta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Airopsis minuta is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create remedies for colds, fever, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Airopsis minuta is a species of grass in the family Restionaceae. It is a genus of small to medium-sized tussock-forming grasses native to Western Australia. The classification of Airopsis minuta places it within the family Restionaceae, which is a group of plants commonly known as restio. This family is further classified under the clade Liliopsida, which includes all monocotyledonous flowering plants. The classification of Airopsis minuta is also linked to the broader clade Angiosperm.
